# UC-18.3.14 · Multi-Vendor Spine-Leaf Topology Anomaly Detection — Unexpected BGP Neighbor Loss, Missing Spine Uplinks, Asymmetric ECMP Path Detection, LLDP/CDP Neighbor Table Drift, Clos Topology Integrity

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep the hall-wide wiring sketch so each rack switch plug lines up with neighbor plugs on paper; when reality slides away long enough, we speak up before packets wander uneven lanes.*

---

## Description

Multi-vendor folded-Clos spine-leaf fabrics depend on every leaf presenting the full mesh of uplinks toward each spine so equal-cost multipath spreads east-west packets evenly. When one uplink disappears silently—often because a BGP finite-state machine stalls outside Established while optics still appear live—the fabric narrows effective bandwidth asymmetrically: hashing concentrates bursts onto surviving uplinks, buffers absorb microbursts, applications experience jitter and TCP retries long before operators assume cabling faults. Spine-leaf topology anomaly detection stitches BGP neighbor posture from NX-OS, EOS, Junos, SONiC, and Cumulus pipelines together with consolidated LLDP adjacency drift signals so operators notice missing spine-facing sessions, forbidden lateral stitch attempts masquerading as fabric peers, MAU timing skew versus permanent unplug events, and uneven ECMP fan-out cues sourced from route summaries paired with interface bandwidth deltas.

## Value

Splunk compares streaming **`bgp:neighbor`** tuples—normalized **eBGP** leaf-spine expectations per **RFC 7938**—against **`expected_bgp_peers.csv`** design rows keyed by **`local_host`**, **`peer_ip`**, **`peer_asn`**, **`link_role`**, and **`expected_state`**, while **`lldp:topology`** snapshots reconcile **`local_chassis`**, **`local_port`**, **`peer_chassis`**, and **`peer_port`** pairs against **`expected_lldp_neighbors.csv`** exported from CMDB or DCIM diagrams. Together these signals shorten war-room debates about whether congestion stems from overlay churn versus silent underlay asymmetry: dashboards spotlight topology drift early, correlate asymmetric ECMP imbalance cues when route buckets diverge across uplinks, exclude **`vPC`** **`peer-link`** and **`MLAG`** bundles via **`mlag_pair_lookup.csv`**, and preserve audit-ready narratives tying anomalies back to racks and spine pods without waiting for manual traceroute drills.

## Implementation

Normalize **`FIELDALIAS`** maps across **`cisco:nxos:bgp`**, **`arista:eos:bgp`**, **`juniper:junos:bgp`**, **`sonic:show:bgp`**, and scripted **`lldp:topology`** collectors; refresh nightly **`expected_bgp_peers.csv`** plus **`expected_lldp_neighbors.csv`** from NetBox or Device42 exports; schedule **`topology_audit_core`** saved searches every five minutes or syslog-triggered supplemental scans.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- **Splunk TA footprint** — Deploy **Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS** ([Splunkbase 2846](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2846)), **Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS** ([Splunkbase 4495](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4495)), and **Splunk Add-on for Juniper** ([Splunkbase 2847](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2847)) on Heavy Forwarders and parsing Indexers; layer optional **Splunk Connect for Syslog** relays where EPS warrants regional fan-in without starving ISSU orchestration bridges elsewhere.
- **Collector lanes** — Stand up scripted modular inputs or Ansible pulls landing normalized sourcetypes (`cisco:nxos:bgp`, `arista:eos:bgp`, `juniper:junos:bgp`, `sonic:show:bgp`) alongside syslog mirrors (`sonic:syslog`, `cumulus:syslog`) plus consolidated `lldp:topology` CSV snapshots emitted from scheduled `show lldp neighbors detail` transcripts paired with inventory identities per chassis serial.
- **Design-of-record lookups** — Maintain `expected_bgp_peers.csv` keyed by **local_host**, **peer_ip**, **peer_asn**, **link_role**, **expected_state** describing **RFC 7938** **eBGP** **leaf_spine** meshes—including optional **three-tier** **super-spine** tuples mirroring superspine pods—and mirror `expected_lldp_neighbors.csv` derived from NetBox or Device42 cabling graphs capturing **local_chassis**, **local_port**, **peer_chassis**, **peer_port** wiring promises aligned with rack elevations.
- **MLAG exclusions** — Populate `mlag_pair_lookup.csv` so legitimate **vPC peer-link**, **MLAG peer-link**, or Ethernet stitches carry mlag markers preventing benign lateral interconnects from appearing as **forbidden_leaf_leaf_suspect** findings during heuristic scans.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) **Cisco Nexus 9000** — Poll **NX-API** JSON exposing BGP neighbors while preserving syslog cues containing **BGP-5-ADJCHANGE** or **LLDP-5-NEIGHBOR_DELETED** mnemonics via SC4S-compatible relays so PRI fidelity survives aggregation tiers before Splunk ingestion timestamps drift correlation windows.
(2) **Arista EOS** — Drive **eAPI** batches emitting **arista:eos:bgp** alongside **OpenConfig** BGP leaves where streaming telemetry budgets exist; normalize **BGP-3-NOTIFICATION** narratives into stable **state** tokens Splunk macros consume downstream without bespoke Rex forks per EOS minor release.
(3) **Juniper QFX** — Combine NETCONF subscriptions feeding **juniper:junos:bgp** with golden transcripts pulled hourly whenever streams lapse—stamp fabric_site tags identical to Apstra intent clusters without blindly merging incompatible schemas into unified sourcetypes until FIELDALIAS parity passes QA gates.
(4) **SONiC** — Pull REST snapshots referencing **bgpcfgd** **APP_DB** neighbors while syslog lanes capture **frr** BGP churn—ensure docker-hosted BGP daemon identities align mgmt hostname conventions mirrored inside **expected_bgp_peers.csv**.
(5) **Cumulus Linux** — Subscribe **cumulus:netq:bgp** gRPC feeds plus **cumulus:syslog** NetQ agent posture—hostname casing mismatches fracture lookups unless nightly reconciliation scripts lowercase mgmt identities consistently across collectors.
(6) **LLDP consolidation** — Aggregate vendor-neutral **lldp:topology** rows capturing peer_role_hint markers distinguishing spine-facing uplinks versus stale orphan descriptions accidentally scraped during partial polls—feed MAU timestamps so suppression macros distinguish **lldp_refresh_skew** jitter from unplug faults during forensic drills.
(7) **Asymmetric ECMP observation lane** — Optional secondary collectors attach route_bucket counters per uplink following **show ip route summary** semantics paired with iface_bw_mbps snapshots—these enrich dashboards spotting routes-per-uplink imbalance cues indicative of uneven BGP advertisement mixes upstream without waiting for overlay dataplane faults highlighted elsewhere.

### Step 2 — Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Publish saved search **topology_audit_core** referencing the fenced SPL below with five-minute cron cadence—or syslog-triggered companions reacting within one minute whenever BGP DOWN or NEIGHBOR_DELETED signatures arrive—while throttle macros suppress paging across overlapping bursts keyed by **local_host** plus **peer_ip** pairs until **sustained_topology_fault** persists three successive buckets aligning with policy demanding **Established** posture beyond five minutes on design-required sessions.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:bgp" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:bgp" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:bgp" OR sourcetype="lldp:topology")
earliest=-6h@m
| eval local_host=trim(coalesce(local_host, hostname, switch_name, device_name, host, ""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, neighbor_address, peer_address, remote_peer, ""))
| eval state=trim(coalesce(state, bgp_state, neighbor_state, sess_state, peer_state, ""))
| eval link_role=lower(trim(coalesce(link_role, session_role, uplink_role, "leaf_spine")))
| lookup expected_bgp_peers.csv local_host peer_ip OUTPUT peer_asn AS design_asn link_role AS design_role expected_state AS design_expect
| eval expected_design_session=if(isnotnull(design_role), 1, null())
| eval state_lower=lower(state)
| eval bgp_fault_non_established=if(sourcetype!="lldp:topology" AND expected_design_session=1 AND state_lower!="established", 1, 0)
| eval local_chassis=trim(coalesce(local_chassis, chassis_id, sys_name, switch_system_name, local_host, ""))
| eval local_port=trim(coalesce(local_port, interface_name, port_name, port_id, ""))
| eval peer_chassis=trim(coalesce(peer_chassis, remote_chassis, neighbor_sys_name, remote_system_name, ""))
| eval peer_port=trim(coalesce(peer_port, neighbor_port_id, remote_port_desc, neighbor_interface, ""))
| eval peer_role_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(peer_role_hint, remote_device_role, "")))
| eval local_role_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(local_role_hint, local_device_role, fabric_role, node_role, "")))
| lookup mlag_pair_lookup.csv local_chassis local_port peer_chassis peer_port OUTPUT mlag_pair_tag AS mlag_exclusion
| eval forbidden_leaf_leaf=if(sourcetype="lldp:topology" AND match(local_role_hint,"leaf|tor") AND match(peer_role_hint,"leaf|tor") AND isnull(mlag_exclusion), "forbidden_leaf_leaf_suspect", null())
| lookup expected_lldp_neighbors.csv local_chassis local_port OUTPUT peer_chassis AS doc_peer_chassis peer_port AS doc_peer_port
| eval lldp_mismatch=if(sourcetype="lldp:topology" AND isnotnull(doc_peer_chassis) AND (peer_chassis!=doc_peer_chassis OR peer_port!=doc_peer_port), "lldp_doc_drift", null())
| eval route_bucket=trim(coalesce(route_bucket, rib_route_count, bgp_prefix_count, ""))
| eval iface_bw_mbps=if(isnotnull(bandwidth_kbps) AND isnull(tonumber(coalesce(iface_bw_mbps, bandwidth_mbps,""))), tonumber(bandwidth_kbps)/1000, tonumber(coalesce(iface_bw_mbps, bandwidth_mbps,"")))
| eval next_hop_hash_groups=tonumber(coalesce(ecmp_paths, nexthop_count, paths, ""))
| stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(state) AS bgp_state latest(bgp_fault_non_established) AS bgp_non_est latest(lldp_mismatch) AS lldp_alarm latest(forbidden_leaf_leaf) AS leaf_leaf_alarm latest(route_bucket) AS routes_seen latest(iface_bw_mbps) AS bw_seen latest(next_hop_hash_groups) AS path_groups BY local_host peer_ip sourcetype
| where bgp_non_est>0 OR isnotnull(lldp_alarm) OR isnotnull(leaf_leaf_alarm)
| sort local_host peer_ip last_seen
| streamstats window=12 global=f latest(bgp_non_est) AS bgp_roll BY local_host peer_ip sourcetype
| eval sustained_topology_fault=if(bgp_roll>=3 AND bgp_non_est>0, "bgp_design_session_not_established_persist", null())
| head 400
| table last_seen local_host peer_ip sourcetype bgp_state sustained_topology_fault lldp_alarm leaf_leaf_alarm routes_seen bw_seen path_groups
```

Correlation narrative — **expected_design_session** restricts BGP compares to curated peers—**bgp_roll** streamstats surfaces chronic stalls—lldp_mismatch highlights DCIM drift—path_groups beside routes_seen exposes uneven ECMP mixes worth escalating toward BGP policy SMEs whenever suspicion survives optics wellness checks executed manually alongside Splunk timelines.

### Step 3 — Validate
SSH parity drills comparing Splunk outputs versus live **NX-OS** **show bgp ipv4 unicast summary**, **EOS** **show bgp neighbors**, **Junos** **show bgp neighbor**, **SONiC** **show ip bgp summary**, and **Cumulus NetQ show bgp neighbor** dashboards establish Splunk extractions mirror CLI finite-state-machine posture within acceptable drift budgets—cross-view NetBox versus actual neighbor descriptions settles residual **lldp_doc_drift** positives referencing mistaken labeling rather than unplug events—confirm **underlay** BGP IPv4 loopback transport posture stays scoped distinctly from overlay BGP-EVPN narratives monitored inside sibling UC **UC-18.3.4** lanes.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard Studio workspace **Underlay Topology Integrity** stacks BGP establishment matrices per spine-facing peer_ip alongside rack_strip charts plotting **lldp_doc_drift** density relative **rack_position** metadata exported from CMDB joins—route overlay severity ribbons annotate ServiceNow CHG overlays referencing cable_relocate_maintenance bundles whenever suppress macros intentionally mute benign churn documented inside ticketing narratives tied **MaintenanceWindow** expansions weekly auditing teams reconcile weekly CSV snapshots archived alongside lineage identifiers proving topology_audit_core executions satisfy inventory assurance reviewers referencing Network_Traffic plus Inventory posture attestations jointly.

### Step 5 — Tuning and extensions
Extend suppression macros honoring CMDB maintenance=true hints plus poap_bootstrap_window tokens capturing zero-touch onboarding timelines **greenfield_leaf_addition** automation lanes intentionally tolerate transient Idle dwell until ASN commissioning completes—three-tier fabrics duplicate lookup scaffolding upward comparing spine superspine peers alongside leaf spine meshes mirroring identical SPL scaffolding albeit taller CSV schemas—alternatively substitute **ospf:adjacency** finite-state equivalents where **OSPF** underlay replaces BGP IPv4 transport following mirrored FIELDALIAS stacks—and correlate prolonged anomalies against UC **UC-18.3.4** **VXLAN** overlay health timelines distinguishing innocent overlay churn absent complementary **underlay** BGP posture breaches.

Governance annex — Anchor terminology reviewers cite quarterly includes **BGP FSM**, **Clos integrity**, **ECMP symmetry**, **fabric_pod tagging**, **ISSU rehearsal**, **graceful_restart_helper**, **design_expect parity**, **route_bucket deltas**, **bw_seen slopes**, **peer_role_hint hygiene**, **split_brain avoidance**, **silent_blackhole mitigation**, **telemetry parity**, **audit readiness**, **pager sobriety**, and **CHG hygiene** documented beside CAB approvals.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:bgp" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:bgp" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:bgp" OR sourcetype="lldp:topology")
earliest=-6h@m
| eval local_host=trim(coalesce(local_host, hostname, switch_name, device_name, host, ""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, neighbor_address, peer_address, remote_peer, ""))
| eval state=trim(coalesce(state, bgp_state, neighbor_state, sess_state, peer_state, ""))
| eval link_role=lower(trim(coalesce(link_role, session_role, uplink_role, "leaf_spine")))
| lookup expected_bgp_peers.csv local_host peer_ip OUTPUT peer_asn AS design_asn link_role AS design_role expected_state AS design_expect
| eval expected_design_session=if(isnotnull(design_role), 1, null())
| eval state_lower=lower(state)
| eval bgp_fault_non_established=if(sourcetype!="lldp:topology" AND expected_design_session=1 AND state_lower!="established", 1, 0)
| eval local_chassis=trim(coalesce(local_chassis, chassis_id, sys_name, switch_system_name, local_host, ""))
| eval local_port=trim(coalesce(local_port, interface_name, port_name, port_id, ""))
| eval peer_chassis=trim(coalesce(peer_chassis, remote_chassis, neighbor_sys_name, remote_system_name, ""))
| eval peer_port=trim(coalesce(peer_port, neighbor_port_id, remote_port_desc, neighbor_interface, ""))
| eval peer_role_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(peer_role_hint, remote_device_role, "")))
| eval local_role_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(local_role_hint, local_device_role, fabric_role, node_role, "")))
| lookup mlag_pair_lookup.csv local_chassis local_port peer_chassis peer_port OUTPUT mlag_pair_tag AS mlag_exclusion
| eval forbidden_leaf_leaf=if(sourcetype="lldp:topology" AND match(local_role_hint,"leaf|tor") AND match(peer_role_hint,"leaf|tor") AND isnull(mlag_exclusion), "forbidden_leaf_leaf_suspect", null())
| lookup expected_lldp_neighbors.csv local_chassis local_port OUTPUT peer_chassis AS doc_peer_chassis peer_port AS doc_peer_port
| eval lldp_mismatch=if(sourcetype="lldp:topology" AND isnotnull(doc_peer_chassis) AND (peer_chassis!=doc_peer_chassis OR peer_port!=doc_peer_port), "lldp_doc_drift", null())
| eval route_bucket=trim(coalesce(route_bucket, rib_route_count, bgp_prefix_count, ""))
| eval iface_bw_mbps=if(isnotnull(bandwidth_kbps) AND isnull(tonumber(coalesce(iface_bw_mbps, bandwidth_mbps,""))), tonumber(bandwidth_kbps)/1000, tonumber(coalesce(iface_bw_mbps, bandwidth_mbps,"")))
| eval next_hop_hash_groups=tonumber(coalesce(ecmp_paths, nexthop_count, paths, ""))
| stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(state) AS bgp_state latest(bgp_fault_non_established) AS bgp_non_est latest(lldp_mismatch) AS lldp_alarm latest(forbidden_leaf_leaf) AS leaf_leaf_alarm latest(route_bucket) AS routes_seen latest(iface_bw_mbps) AS bw_seen latest(next_hop_hash_groups) AS path_groups BY local_host peer_ip sourcetype
| where bgp_non_est>0 OR isnotnull(lldp_alarm) OR isnotnull(leaf_leaf_alarm)
| sort local_host peer_ip last_seen
| streamstats window=12 global=f latest(bgp_non_est) AS bgp_roll BY local_host peer_ip sourcetype
| eval sustained_topology_fault=if(bgp_roll>=3 AND bgp_non_est>0, "bgp_design_session_not_established_persist", null())
| head 400
| table last_seen local_host peer_ip sourcetype bgp_state sustained_topology_fault lldp_alarm leaf_leaf_alarm routes_seen bw_seen path_groups
```

## Visualization

(1) Matrix heat-map — **`local_host`** × spine **`peer_ip`** colored by **`Established`** versus stuck **FSM** states. (2) Rack lane strip — **`lldp_doc_drift`** counts per **`local_chassis`**. (3) Timeline — BGP **`Idle`**/**`Connect`** dwell stacked beside **`MaintenanceWindow`** overlays. (4) Table — **`forbidden_leaf_leaf_suspect`** rows enriched with **`mlag_exclusion`** flags. (5) Scatter — **`routes_seen`** versus **`bw_seen`** per uplink highlighting asymmetric ECMP suspicion.

## Known False Positives

**spine_pod_expansion_window** — Extra spine turns up with uplinks Idle until optics seat—suppress via spine_pod_expansion_window CMDB tags plus staged-spine CHG tickets instead of paging topology_integrity_hit until cabling milestones finish.

**greenfield_leaf_addition** — Collectors arrive before expected_bgp_peers.csv catches new hostnames—brief absence races provisioning—stamp greenfield_leaf_addition referencing poap_bootstrap_window until design_expect parity closes.

**bgp_graceful_restart_helper** — Spine ISSU keeps forwarding warm while BGP stalls helper-side—sessions diverge briefly from Established—suppress bgp_design_session_not_established_persist when MaintenanceWindow cites ISSU rehearsal CHGs plus soak timers.

**lldp_refresh_skew** — EOS versus NX-OS TTL cadences produce momentary neighbor gaps shorter than unplug faults—raise dwell thresholds before lldp_doc_drift pages unless optics-loss syslog corroborates.

**vpc_peer_link_misclassified** — Without mlag_pair_lookup.csv reloads, vPC peer-link or MLAG stitches resemble forbidden lateral pairs—reload ServiceNow-fed lookups before paging forbidden_leaf_leaf_suspect lacking mlag_exclusion.

**cable_relocate_maintenance** — Documented spine patch moves flip sessions DOWN then Established—suppress until cable_relocate_maintenance CHG closure aligns with MaintenanceWindow cmdb peer_ip relocation notes.

**poap_bootstrap_window** — ZTP bootstrap lets Sonic or Cumulus identities oscillate until ASN stamps finalize—pair with greenfield_leaf_addition so Idle dwell never reads as spine loss.

## References

- [IETF RFC 7938 — Use of BGP for Routing in Large-Scale Data Centers](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7938)
